rawResults.summary: This Solar Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) establishes a 20-year contract (with up to three 5-year extensions) under which the Purchaser agrees to buy all electricity generated by the Seller's solar system at specified rates. The Seller retains ownership of the system and associated renewable energy credits (RECs), while the Purchaser is responsible for paying monthly invoices based on actual energy production and certain taxes.

keyTerms.termLength: Twenty (20) years initial term with up to three (3) additional five (5) year terms
keyTerms.pricePerKwh: Not specified (to be filled in Exhibit 1 Pricing table)
keyTerms.terminationClause: Termination allowed prior to installation under certain conditions; Purchaser may terminate for delay if installation does not commence within a specified number of days; termination payments apply as per Exhibit 1 and Exhibit 3 Section 11(b)

riskFlags.issue: The contract does not specify any escalation rate for the price per kWh over the 20-year term, which may expose the Purchaser to price uncertainty or the Seller to inflation risk.
riskFlags.section: Exhibit 1 Pricing, Section 3

riskFlags.issue: The Performance Guaranty is optional and may not be provided, which could leave the Purchaser without assurance of system performance or recourse if the system underperforms.
riskFlags.section: Exhibit 1 Pricing, Section 4(d)
riskFlags.evidence: d. A Performance Guaranty is _____ is not_____ being provided.

riskFlags.issue: The termination payment amounts for each contract year are left blank, creating uncertainty about the financial consequences of early termination.
riskFlags.section: Exhibit 1 Pricing, Section 6

riskFlags.issue: Purchaser is responsible for all taxes related to generation, sale, delivery, or consumption of electricity and real property taxes, which could increase costs unexpectedly.
riskFlags.section: Exhibit 3 General Terms and Conditions, Section 3(d)(i)
riskFlags.evidence: Purchaser is responsible for: (1) payment of, or reimbursement of Seller, for all taxes assessed on the generation, sale, delivery or consumption of electricity produced by the System or the interconnection of the System to the utility’s electricity distribution system; and (2) real property taxes.

riskFlags.term: Seller Retains Ownership of RECs and Incentives
riskFlags.issue: Seller retains all rights to Renewable Energy Credits (RECs) and incentives, which may limit Purchaser's ability to claim environmental benefits or use credits for compliance or marketing.
riskFlags.section: Exhibit 3 General Terms and Conditions, Section 4
riskFlags.evidence: As the owner of the System, Seller is entitled to the benefit of, and will retain all ownership interests in the RECs and Incentives. Purchaser shall cooperate with Seller in obtaining, securing and transferring any and all RECs and Incentives.

riskFlags.issue: The contract does not specify the system size or detailed description, which may lead to ambiguity about the expected energy production and system scope.

rawResults.summary: This Solar Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) establishes a 20-year contract (with up to three 5-year extensions) under which the Purchaser agrees to buy all electricity generated by the Seller's solar system at specified rates. The agreement outlines responsibilities for installation, operation, maintenance, billing, taxes, and ownership of renewable energy credits (RECs), with detailed terms on termination and pricing assumptions.

keyTerms.termLength: Twenty (20) years initial term, with up to three (3) additional five (5) year terms
keyTerms.capacity: System Size (DC kW) - Not specified in provided text
keyTerms.pricePerKwh: Contract Year $/kWh - Not specified in provided text
keyTerms.omResponsibility: Seller responsible for installation, operation, and maintenance (implied in Section 6, not fully shown)
keyTerms.terminationClause: Termination allowed prior to installation under certain conditions; Purchaser may terminate for delay; termination payments specified in Exhibit 1, Section 6

riskFlags.issue: The contract does not specify any escalation rate for the contract price over the 20-year term, which may expose the Purchaser to unexpected price increases or lack of price adjustment for inflation.
riskFlags.section: Exhibit 1, Pricing

riskFlags.issue: The Performance Guaranty is optional and may not be provided, which could leave the Purchaser without assurance of system performance or remedies if the system underperforms.
riskFlags.section: Exhibit 1, Pricing, Section 4(d)
riskFlags.evidence: d. A Performance Guaranty is _____ is not_____ being provided.

riskFlags.issue: Termination payments are required if the agreement is terminated early, but the amounts are unspecified, potentially exposing the Purchaser to significant financial penalties.
riskFlags.section: Exhibit 1, Pricing, Section 6

riskFlags.issue: Purchaser is responsible for all taxes related to generation, sale, delivery, or consumption of electricity and real property taxes, which could result in unexpected tax liabilities.
riskFlags.section: Exhibit 3, General Terms and Conditions, Section 3(d)
riskFlags.evidence:
d. Taxes.
  i. Purchaser’s Taxes.    Purchaser  is  responsible  for:  (1)  payment  of,  or  reimbursement  of  Seller,  for  all  taxes 
assessed  on  the  generation,  sale,  delivery  or  consumption  of  electricity  produced  by  the  System  or  the 
interconnection of the System to the utility’s electricity distribution system; and (2) real property taxes.

riskFlags.issue: Seller retains ownership of all Renewable Energy Credits (RECs) and incentives, which may limit Purchaser's ability to claim environmental benefits or use RECs for compliance or marketing.
riskFlags.section: Exhibit 3, General Terms and Conditions, Section 4
riskFlags.evidence:
4. RECs and Incentives.  As the owner of the System, Seller is entitled to the benefit of, and will retain all ownership interests 
in the RECs and Incentives. Purchaser shall cooperate with Seller in obtaining, securing and transferring any and all RECs and 
Incentives. Purchaser is not  obligated  to incur any  out–of–pocket costs  or  expenses in connection with such actions unless 
reimbursed by Seller.

riskFlags.issue: Purchaser may terminate if installation does not commence within a specified number of days after the Effective Date, but the exact number of days is left blank, creating uncertainty about the Purchaser's rights.
riskFlags.section: Exhibit 3, General Terms and Conditions, Section 2(d)
riskFlags.evidence:
d. Termination by Purchaser for Delay.   If Commencement  of Installation has not occurred [_____] days after the 
Effective Date, Purchaser may terminate this Agreement by providing thirty (30) days’ prior written notice to Seller; 
provided that this Agreement will not terminate pursuant to this Section 2(d) if Seller achieves Commencement  of 
Installation on or before the end of such thirty (30) day notice period.

riskFlags.term: System Size and Description Not Specified
riskFlags.issue: The contract does not specify the system size or detailed system description, which may cause ambiguity about the expected system capacity and performance.

rawResults.summary: This Solar Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) establishes a 20-year initial term for the purchase and sale of electricity generated by a solar system installed on the Purchaser's premises, with options for up to three additional 5-year terms. The Purchaser agrees to pay the Seller a specified price per kWh of electricity generated, with detailed terms on billing, taxes, and ownership of renewable energy credits (RECs). The agreement includes provisions for termination, performance guarantees, and responsibilities for operation and maintenance.

keyTerms.termLength: Twenty (20) years initial term with up to three (3) additional five (5) year terms
keyTerms.pricePerKwh: Contract Year $/kWh: Not specified (to be filled in Exhibit 1)
keyTerms.terminationClause: Termination allowed prior to installation under certain conditions; Purchaser may terminate for delay if installation does not commence within a specified number of days; termination payments specified in Exhibit 1
